page: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

For local storage, the instance includes 1900 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed local read and write capabilities directly attached to the Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps. The system lacks dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on CPU-based execution for all computational tasks.

Benchmark data highlights strong performance in single-core and multi-core operations. The instance achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2205.0 and a strong multi-core score of 18417.0. In text and image processing, it registers top-tier results, alongside a strong Passmark CPU score of 51792.67. Memory bandwidth tests show a strong read-only large block transfer rate of 180.41 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the CPU's AMX and AVX capabilities support strong performance in small to medium LLM inference, reaching 371.41 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model and 26.66 tokens/sec on a 7B model.

Given its compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2 and the inclusion of fast local NVMe storage, the c8id.8xlarge offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring high CPU cycles and rapid local I/O without the overhead of network-attached storage. It is highly suited for compute-heavy applications, database operations, high-throughput web serving, and localized data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring a dedicated 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed via the Nitro hypervisor. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2205.0, and strong multi-core capabilities, including a Passmark CPU score of 51792.67. The instance delivers strong memory bandwidth and high-throughput static web serving at 57.59 GB/sec. Lacking a GPU, it relies on its high-frequency CPU and fast local storage to drive performance. This configuration is well-suited for high-performance computing, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.
